topic: A button appeared in my office elevator for a floor the building does not have.
title: The Button For A Floor That Doesn't Exist | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My elevator grew a button overnight, for a floor my building does not have.
scenes.image_prompt: Close still of an elevator control panel, dim warm bulb, one extra unlabeled button glowing faintly below the rest, deep shadow, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I work on the fourteenth floor of an old downtown tower.
scenes.image_prompt: Empty office tower lobby at dusk, polished floor reflecting amber light, brass elevator doors closed, low light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Same ride, same hour, every single morning for years.
scenes.image_prompt: Interior of an empty elevator, brushed steel walls, fluorescent ceiling panel buzzing, faint grain, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: That morning the panel had a number that should not exist. Thirteen.
scenes.image_prompt: Elevator button panel detail, a worn button marked thirteen lit dim red, scuffed metal, harsh overhead light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: My building skips thirteen. Always has.
scenes.image_prompt: Old building floor directory plate on a wall, numbers jumping from twelve to fourteen, dust, dim corridor light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I checked the directory in the lobby. It still skipped that number.
scenes.image_prompt: Lobby wall directory board, mounted letters and numbers, gap where thirteen should be, soft fluorescent glow, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: So one evening, alone, I pressed it.
scenes.image_prompt: A hand reaching toward a glowing elevator button in shadow, fingertip almost touching, harsh single light source, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The doors closed. The elevator dropped. The numbers above counted down past one.
scenes.image_prompt: Elevator floor indicator above doors, glowing numerals descending below one into blank, dark interior, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: It kept dropping. Far longer than any basement could be.
scenes.image_prompt: Dark elevator shaft seen through a gap in doors, endless cables vanishing downward, faint emergency light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The doors opened on a hallway identical to mine, but every nameplate was mine.
scenes.image_prompt: Long office corridor lined with identical doors, every door plate blank and pale, flickering fluorescent tubes, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I mashed every button until it carried me back up.
scenes.image_prompt: Elevator panel with all buttons lit at once, trembling hand pulled back, stark white light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: This morning the button was gone. But my desk upstairs felt slightly warm.
scenes.image_prompt: An empty office desk by a window at dawn, chair pushed slightly out, faint indentation in the seat, grey light, vertical 9:16 framing
